seo.title: AG2 Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for AG2 agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams using AG2 for multi-agent legal research and drafting face heightened risks due to the framework's conversational nature, which can lead to off-topic drifts, factual errors in group consensus, or hallucinated case citations. AG2's group chat and code execution features amplify dangers like breaching attorney-client privilege or violating bar ethics rules. Vex provides essential guardrails to ensure compliance with strict legal regulations and prevent catastrophic failures like malpractice suits.

complianceRequirements.description: All client confidential information must remain protected and never exposed in agent conversations or logs.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex message hooks automatically redact PII and privileged content before processing, flagging potential breaches for attorney review.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal content must be reviewed by a licensed attorney before use in filings or advice.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces human-in-the-loop approval workflows, blocking un-reviewed outputs from reaching production.

complianceRequirements.regulation: Citations must reference real, verifiable cases
complianceRequirements.description: All case citations must link to actual, verifiable legal precedents; fabricated citations are prohibited.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates citations against legal databases via message hooks, rejecting hallucinated references like Mata v. Avianca.

complianceRequirements.description: AI processing of discovery materials must preserve chain of custody and metadata integrity.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all agent interactions immutably while preventing external data exfiltration or model training usage.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Agents generate fake case names and rulings that appear authoritative.
industryRisks.example: AG2 group chat cites 'Mata v. Avianca (2023)' as binding precedent, leading to court sanctions.

industryRisks.name: Privilege breach in group chat
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent conversations inadvertently expose client confidential details.
industryRisks.example: Research agent shares client SSN in consensus discussion, violating attorney-client privilege.

industryRisks.name: Unreviewed AI legal advice
industryRisks.description: Agent outputs bypass attorney review and enter client communications.
industryRisks.example: Drafting agent produces contract clause filed without human verification, triggering malpractice claim.

industryRisks.name: Speaker selection bias
industryRisks.description: Group chat manager favors inaccurate agents, reaching wrong legal consensus.
industryRisks.example: Biased selection amplifies junior agent's incorrect statute interpretation over senior agent.

implementation.title: Register Vex Message Hook for Privilege Protection
implementation.description: Install Vex and register as AG2 message hook to automatically redact PII and privileged content from all agent messages.

implementation.title: Enforce Human Review for Legal Outputs
implementation.description: Block AI-generated legal content from final output until licensed attorney approves via Vex workflow.

implementation.title: Prevent Off-Topic Drift and Consensus Errors
implementation.description: Monitor conversation drift and validate group consensus against legal facts using Vex semantic analysis.

caseStudyScenario.heading: AG2 in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized law firm deploys AG2 to accelerate contract dispute research. Three agents collaborate: a case law researcher, a statute analyzer, and a drafting assistant. The group chat begins analyzing a breach of contract claim involving a SaaS provider. The researcher agent pulls relevant precedents, the statute agent cross-references UCC provisions, and the drafter synthesizes arguments for a motion to dismiss.

Without guardrails, the conversation spirals when the researcher hallucinates 'VexCorp v. CloudSys (2024)' as controlling precedent. The group chat manager selects this agent repeatedly due to confident tone, leading consensus around the fake case. The drafting agent produces a motion citing the fabricated precedent, which junior associates file without review. Client confidential details about pricing disputes leak into the group chat logs.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex message hooks registered, the first hallucinated citation triggers immediate rejection: 'CITATION INVALID - REQUIRES ATTORNEY REVIEW.' PII in pricing discussion gets redacted automatically. Conversation drift toward unrelated IP law gets corrected. The senior partner receives a review ticket with cleaned outputs, verifies real precedents (actual SaaS cases like 'SaaS Inc. v. VendorX'), and approves sanitized content. The motion succeeds without sanctions, privilege remains intact, and the firm avoids the Mata v. Avianca-style embarrassment.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and register message hooks to protect your AG2 legal deployments from compliance failures.
